[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11537864]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Multiple issues| {{more citations needed|date=February 2024}} {{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=桜井神社 (堺市)|date=December 2021}} }}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | native_name = 桜井神社 | native_name_lang = ja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sakurai-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Worship Hall ([[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]) | location = 645 Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami Ward]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] 590-0121 | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | festival = First Sunday of October | country = {{flag|Japan}} | type = Former [[Modern_system_of_Ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|Ken-sha]] | prefecture= {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1041996}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.485417|N|135.506167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, ''Sakurai jinja'', also 櫻井神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It was founded at an unknown date and holds its festival on the first Sunday in October. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] as [[kami]]. The oratory (拝殿, ''haiden'') is designated as the only [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|Japanese National Treasure]] in Sakai City.{{Cite web |title=Temples & Shrines |url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/foreign-language/english/visitors/enjoying/temples_shirines.html |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=www.city.sakai.lg.jp |language=}}{{Cite web |title=Sakurai Shrine |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/spot/sakuraijinja |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=OSAKA INFO |language=}} {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|260px]]
Worship Hall (National Treasure) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=260}} | location = Katakura 645, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] | Position = {{coord|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | alternate name = Kamitani Hachiman Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = First Sunday of October | ceremony = }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine Gate}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, also 櫻井神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha|shrine listed in the Engishiki]] and was formerly classified as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is also known as "Kamitani Hachiman Shrine." The worship hall is the only [[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]] in Sakai City. == Deities == The current deities enshrined are as followsShrine History.: * Central Hall: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] - the 15th emperor. * East Hall: [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] - the 14th emperor. * West Hall: [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] - consort of Emperor Chūai. == History == According to legend, the Sakurai clan, who lived in this area, enshrined their ancestor [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], marking the shrine's origin. In the 5th year of the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (597), the shrine incorporated a Hachiman shrine. In the [[Engishiki]] Jinmyōchō (927), the shrine is listed as "Sakurai Shrine" in [[Ōtori District]], [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], indicating its status as a minor shrine. The shrine also later incorporated Yama no I Shrine and Kuni Shrine (a small shrine still exists near [[Hōdōji Temple]]), both listed in the Engishiki. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] progressed, leading to the construction of Kiyuzan Jingū-ji Temple within the shrine grounds. In the 4th year of the Kenmu era (1337), Kamitani Jōsu built a shrine building. In the 5th year of the [[Tenshō era]] (1577), the [[Siege of Kishū]] by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] resulted in the destruction of all buildings except the worship hall, and the confiscation of shrine lands. The reconstruction began in the 16th year of Tenshō (1588) by [[:en:Katō Kiyomasa|Katō Kiyomasa]], starting with the Amida Hall, and continued through the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1688–1704). In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1868), during the [[Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order]], Buddhist images and scriptures were moved to Konpuku-ji Temple in Katakura, and the Amida Hall was relocated to Hokke-ji Temple in Bessho. The bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storage building. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. Between 1908 (Meiji 41) and 1910 (Meiji 43), the shrine incorporated nine other shrines, including Kuni Shrine. In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Izumigaoka Station|Izumigaoka Station]], take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", [[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]], 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}}

In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Izumigaoka Station|Izumigaoka Station]], take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", [[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]], 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537864]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Multiple issues| {{more citations needed|date=February 2024}} {{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=桜井神社 (堺市)|date=December 2021}} }}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | native_name = 桜井神社 | native_name_lang = ja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sakurai-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Worship Hall ([[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]) | location = 645 Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami Ward]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] 590-0121 | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | festival = First Sunday of October | country = {{flag|Japan}} | type = Former [[Modern_system_of_Ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|Ken-sha]] | prefecture= {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1041996}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.485417|N|135.506167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, ''Sakurai jinja'', also 櫻井神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It was founded at an unknown date and holds its festival on the first Sunday in October. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] as [[kami]]. The oratory (拝殿, ''haiden'') is designated as the only [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|Japanese National Treasure]] in Sakai City.{{Cite web |title=Temples & Shrines |url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/foreign-language/english/visitors/enjoying/temples_shirines.html |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=www.city.sakai.lg.jp |language=}}{{Cite web |title=Sakurai Shrine |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/spot/sakuraijinja |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=OSAKA INFO |language=}} {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|260px]]
Worship Hall (National Treasure) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=260}} | location = Katakura 645, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] | Position = {{coord|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | alternate name = Kamitani Hachiman Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = First Sunday of October | ceremony = }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine Gate}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, also 櫻井神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha|shrine listed in the Engishiki]] and was formerly classified as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is also known as "Kamitani Hachiman Shrine." The worship hall is the only [[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]] in Sakai City. == Deities == The current deities enshrined are as followsShrine History.: * Central Hall: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] - the 15th emperor. * East Hall: [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] - the 14th emperor. * West Hall: [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] - consort of Emperor Chūai. == History == According to legend, the Sakurai clan, who lived in this area, enshrined their ancestor [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], marking the shrine's origin. In the 5th year of the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (597), the shrine incorporated a Hachiman shrine. In the [[Engishiki]] Jinmyōchō (927), the shrine is listed as "Sakurai Shrine" in [[Ōtori District]], [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], indicating its status as a minor shrine. The shrine also later incorporated Yama no I Shrine and Kuni Shrine (a small shrine still exists near [[Hōdōji Temple]]), both listed in the Engishiki. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] progressed, leading to the construction of Kiyuzan Jingū-ji Temple within the shrine grounds. In the 4th year of the Kenmu era (1337), Kamitani Jōsu built a shrine building. In the 5th year of the [[Tenshō era]] (1577), the [[Siege of Kishū]] by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] resulted in the destruction of all buildings except the worship hall, and the confiscation of shrine lands. The reconstruction began in the 16th year of Tenshō (1588) by [[:en:Katō Kiyomasa|Katō Kiyomasa]], starting with the Amida Hall, and continued through the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1688–1704). In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1868), during the [[Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order]], Buddhist images and scriptures were moved to Konpuku-ji Temple in Katakura, and the Amida Hall was relocated to Hokke-ji Temple in Bessho. The bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storage building. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. Between 1908 (Meiji 41) and 1910 (Meiji 43), the shrine incorporated nine other shrines, including Kuni Shrine. In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Izumigaoka Station|Izumigaoka Station]], take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", [[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]], 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Izumigaoka Station|Izumigaoka Station]], take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", [[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]], 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537864]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Multiple issues| {{more citations needed|date=February 2024}} {{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=桜井神社 (堺市)|date=December 2021}} }}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | native_name = 桜井神社 | native_name_lang = ja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sakurai-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Worship Hall ([[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]) | location = 645 Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami Ward]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] 590-0121 | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | festival = First Sunday of October | country = {{flag|Japan}} | type = Former [[Modern_system_of_Ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|Ken-sha]] | prefecture= {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1041996}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.485417|N|135.506167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, ''Sakurai jinja'', also 櫻井神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It was founded at an unknown date and holds its festival on the first Sunday in October. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] as [[kami]]. The oratory (拝殿, ''haiden'') is designated as the only [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|Japanese National Treasure]] in Sakai City.{{Cite web |title=Temples & Shrines |url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/foreign-language/english/visitors/enjoying/temples_shirines.html |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=www.city.sakai.lg.jp |language=}}{{Cite web |title=Sakurai Shrine |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/spot/sakuraijinja |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=OSAKA INFO |language=}} {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|260px]]
Worship Hall (National Treasure) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=260}} | location = Katakura 645, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] | Position = {{coord|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | alternate name = Kamitani Hachiman Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = First Sunday of October | ceremony = }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine Gate}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, also 櫻井神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha|shrine listed in the Engishiki]] and was formerly classified as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is also known as "Kamitani Hachiman Shrine." The worship hall is the only [[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]] in Sakai City. == Deities == The current deities enshrined are as followsShrine History.: * Central Hall: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] - the 15th emperor. * East Hall: [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] - the 14th emperor. * West Hall: [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] - consort of Emperor Chūai. == History == According to legend, the Sakurai clan, who lived in this area, enshrined their ancestor [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], marking the shrine's origin. In the 5th year of the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (597), the shrine incorporated a Hachiman shrine. In the [[Engishiki]] Jinmyōchō (927), the shrine is listed as "Sakurai Shrine" in [[Ōtori District]], [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], indicating its status as a minor shrine. The shrine also later incorporated Yama no I Shrine and Kuni Shrine (a small shrine still exists near [[Hōdōji Temple]]), both listed in the Engishiki. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] progressed, leading to the construction of Kiyuzan Jingū-ji Temple within the shrine grounds. In the 4th year of the Kenmu era (1337), Kamitani Jōsu built a shrine building. In the 5th year of the [[Tenshō era]] (1577), the [[Siege of Kishū]] by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] resulted in the destruction of all buildings except the worship hall, and the confiscation of shrine lands. The reconstruction began in the 16th year of Tenshō (1588) by [[:en:Katō Kiyomasa|Katō Kiyomasa]], starting with the Amida Hall, and continued through the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1688–1704). In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1868), during the [[Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order]], Buddhist images and scriptures were moved to Konpuku-ji Temple in Katakura, and the Amida Hall was relocated to Hokke-ji Temple in Bessho. The bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storage building. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. Between 1908 (Meiji 41) and 1910 (Meiji 43), the shrine incorporated nine other shrines, including Kuni Shrine. In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Izumigaoka Station|Izumigaoka Station]], take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", [[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]], 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Izumigaoka Station|Izumigaoka Station]], take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", [[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]], 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537864]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Multiple issues| {{more citations needed|date=February 2024}} {{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=桜井神社 (堺市)|date=December 2021}} }}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | native_name = 桜井神社 | native_name_lang = ja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sakurai-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Worship Hall ([[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]) | location = 645 Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami Ward]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] 590-0121 | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | festival = First Sunday of October | country = {{flag|Japan}} | type = Former [[Modern_system_of_Ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|Ken-sha]] | prefecture= {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1041996}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.485417|N|135.506167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, ''Sakurai jinja'', also 櫻井神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It was founded at an unknown date and holds its festival on the first Sunday in October. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] as [[kami]]. The oratory (拝殿, ''haiden'') is designated as the only [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|Japanese National Treasure]] in Sakai City.{{Cite web |title=Temples & Shrines |url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/foreign-language/english/visitors/enjoying/temples_shirines.html |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=www.city.sakai.lg.jp |language=}}{{Cite web |title=Sakurai Shrine |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/spot/sakuraijinja |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=OSAKA INFO |language=}} {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|260px]]
Worship Hall (National Treasure) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=260}} | location = Katakura 645, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] | Position = {{coord|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | alternate name = Kamitani Hachiman Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = First Sunday of October | ceremony = }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine Gate}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, also 櫻井神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha|shrine listed in the Engishiki]] and was formerly classified as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is also known as "Kamitani Hachiman Shrine." The worship hall is the only [[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]] in Sakai City. == Deities == The current deities enshrined are as followsShrine History.: * Central Hall: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] - the 15th emperor. * East Hall: [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] - the 14th emperor. * West Hall: [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] - consort of Emperor Chūai. == History == According to legend, the Sakurai clan, who lived in this area, enshrined their ancestor [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], marking the shrine's origin. In the 5th year of the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (597), the shrine incorporated a Hachiman shrine. In the [[Engishiki]] Jinmyōchō (927), the shrine is listed as "Sakurai Shrine" in [[Ōtori District]], [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], indicating its status as a minor shrine. The shrine also later incorporated Yama no I Shrine and Kuni Shrine (a small shrine still exists near [[Hōdōji Temple]]), both listed in the Engishiki. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] progressed, leading to the construction of Kiyuzan Jingū-ji Temple within the shrine grounds. In the 4th year of the Kenmu era (1337), Kamitani Jōsu built a shrine building. In the 5th year of the [[Tenshō era]] (1577), the [[Siege of Kishū]] by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] resulted in the destruction of all buildings except the worship hall, and the confiscation of shrine lands. The reconstruction began in the 16th year of Tenshō (1588) by [[:en:Katō Kiyomasa|Katō Kiyomasa]], starting with the Amida Hall, and continued through the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1688–1704). In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1868), during the [[Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order]], Buddhist images and scriptures were moved to Konpuku-ji Temple in Katakura, and the Amida Hall was relocated to Hokke-ji Temple in Bessho. The bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storage building. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. Between 1908 (Meiji 41) and 1910 (Meiji 43), the shrine incorporated nine other shrines, including Kuni Shrine. In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Izumigaoka Station|Izumigaoka Station]], take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", [[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]], 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Izumigaoka Station|Izumigaoka Station]], take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", [[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]], 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537864]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Multiple issues| {{more citations needed|date=February 2024}} {{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=桜井神社 (堺市)|date=December 2021}} }}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | native_name = 桜井神社 | native_name_lang = ja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sakurai-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Worship Hall ([[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]) | location = 645 Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami Ward]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] 590-0121 | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | festival = First Sunday of October | country = {{flag|Japan}} | type = Former [[Modern_system_of_Ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|Ken-sha]] | prefecture= {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1041996}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.485417|N|135.506167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, ''Sakurai jinja'', also 櫻井神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It was founded at an unknown date and holds its festival on the first Sunday in October. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] as [[kami]]. The oratory (拝殿, ''haiden'') is designated as the only [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|Japanese National Treasure]] in Sakai City.{{Cite web |title=Temples & Shrines |url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/foreign-language/english/visitors/enjoying/temples_shirines.html |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=www.city.sakai.lg.jp |language=}}{{Cite web |title=Sakurai Shrine |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/spot/sakuraijinja |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=OSAKA INFO |language=}} {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|260px]]
Worship Hall (National Treasure) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=260}} | location = Katakura 645, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] | Position = {{coord|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | alternate name = Kamitani Hachiman Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = First Sunday of October | ceremony = }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine Gate}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, also 櫻井神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha|shrine listed in the Engishiki]] and was formerly classified as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is also known as "Kamitani Hachiman Shrine." The worship hall is the only [[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]] in Sakai City. == Deities == The current deities enshrined are as followsShrine History.: * Central Hall: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] - the 15th emperor. * East Hall: [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] - the 14th emperor. * West Hall: [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] - consort of Emperor Chūai. == History == According to legend, the Sakurai clan, who lived in this area, enshrined their ancestor [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], marking the shrine's origin. In the 5th year of the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (597), the shrine incorporated a Hachiman shrine. In the [[Engishiki]] Jinmyōchō (927), the shrine is listed as "Sakurai Shrine" in [[Ōtori District]], [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], indicating its status as a minor shrine. The shrine also later incorporated Yama no I Shrine and Kuni Shrine (a small shrine still exists near [[Hōdōji Temple]]), both listed in the Engishiki. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] progressed, leading to the construction of Kiyuzan Jingū-ji Temple within the shrine grounds. In the 4th year of the Kenmu era (1337), Kamitani Jōsu built a shrine building. In the 5th year of the [[Tenshō era]] (1577), the [[Siege of Kishū]] by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] resulted in the destruction of all buildings except the worship hall, and the confiscation of shrine lands. The reconstruction began in the 16th year of Tenshō (1588) by [[:en:Katō Kiyomasa|Katō Kiyomasa]], starting with the Amida Hall, and continued through the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1688–1704). In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1868), during the [[Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order]], Buddhist images and scriptures were moved to Konpuku-ji Temple in Katakura, and the Amida Hall was relocated to Hokke-ji Temple in Bessho. The bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storage building. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. Between 1908 (Meiji 41) and 1910 (Meiji 43), the shrine incorporated nine other shrines, including Kuni Shrine. In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Izumigaoka Station|Izumigaoka Station]], take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", [[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]], 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] }} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537864]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Multiple issues| {{more citations needed|date=February 2024}} {{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=桜井神社 (堺市)|date=December 2021}} }}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | native_name = 桜井神社 | native_name_lang = ja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sakurai-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Worship Hall ([[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|National Treasure]]) | location = 645 Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami Ward]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka]] 590-0121 | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | festival = First Sunday of October | country = {{flag|Japan}} | type = Former [[Modern_system_of_Ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|Ken-sha]] | prefecture= {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1041996}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.485417|N|135.506167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, ''Sakurai jinja'', also 櫻井神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It was founded at an unknown date and holds its festival on the first Sunday in October. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] as [[kami]]. The oratory (拝殿, ''haiden'') is designated as the only [[:en:National Treasure (Japan)|Japanese National Treasure]] in Sakai City.{{Cite web |title=Temples & Shrines |url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/foreign-language/english/visitors/enjoying/temples_shirines.html |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=www.city.sakai.lg.jp |language=}}{{Cite web |title=Sakurai Shrine |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/spot/sakuraijinja |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=OSAKA INFO |language=}} {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|260px]]
Worship Hall (National Treasure) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=260}} | location = Katakura 645, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] | Position = {{coord|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | alternate name = Kamitani Hachiman Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = First Sunday of October | ceremony = }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine Gate}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, also 櫻井神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Katakura, [[:en:Minami-ku, Sakai|Minami-ku]], [[:en:Sakai|Sakai]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a [[Shikinaisha|shrine listed in the Engishiki]] and was formerly classified as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is also known as "Kamitani Hachiman Shrine." The worship hall is the only [[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]] in Sakai City. == Deities == The current deities enshrined are as followsShrine History.: * Central Hall: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] - the 15th emperor. * East Hall: [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] - the 14th emperor. * West Hall: [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] - consort of Emperor Chūai. == History == According to legend, the Sakurai clan, who lived in this area, enshrined their ancestor [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], marking the shrine's origin. In the 5th year of the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] (597), the shrine incorporated a Hachiman shrine. In the [[Engishiki]] Jinmyōchō (927), the shrine is listed as "Sakurai Shrine" in [[Ōtori District]], [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]], indicating its status as a minor shrine. The shrine also later incorporated Yama no I Shrine and Kuni Shrine (a small shrine still exists near [[Hōdōji Temple]]), both listed in the Engishiki. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] progressed, leading to the construction of Kiyuzan Jingū-ji Temple within the shrine grounds. In the 4th year of the Kenmu era (1337), Kamitani Jōsu built a shrine building. In the 5th year of the [[Tenshō era]] (1577), the [[Siege of Kishū]] by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] resulted in the destruction of all buildings except the worship hall, and the confiscation of shrine lands. The reconstruction began in the 16th year of Tenshō (1588) by [[:en:Katō Kiyomasa|Katō Kiyomasa]], starting with the Amida Hall, and continued through the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1688–1704). In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1868), during the [[Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order]], Buddhist images and scriptures were moved to Konpuku-ji Temple in Katakura, and the Amida Hall was relocated to Hokke-ji Temple in Bessho. The bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storage building. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. Between 1908 (Meiji 41) and 1910 (Meiji 43), the shrine incorporated nine other shrines, including Kuni Shrine. In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] [[:en:Izumigaoka Station|Izumigaoka Station]], take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", [[:en:The Asahi Shimbun|The Asahi Shimbun]], 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}}
