[[co:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537864]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine in Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Multiple issues| {{more citations needed|date=February 2024}} {{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=桜井神社 (堺市)|date=December 2021}} }}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | native_name = 桜井神社 | native_name_lang = ja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sakurai-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Worship Hall ({{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}) | location = 645 Katakura, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka}} 590-0121 | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | festival = First Sunday of October | country = {{flag|Japan}} | type = Former [[Modern_system_of_Ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|Ken-sha]] | prefecture= {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1041996}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.485417|N|135.506167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, ''Sakurai jinja'', also 櫻井神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It was founded at an unknown date and holds its festival on the first Sunday in October. It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} as [[kami]]. The oratory (拝殿, ''haiden'') is designated as the only {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=Japanese National Treasure}} in Sakai City.{{Cite web |title=Temples & Shrines |url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/foreign-language/english/visitors/enjoying/temples_shirines.html |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=www.city.sakai.lg.jp |language=}}{{Cite web |title=Sakurai Shrine |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/spot/sakuraijinja |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=OSAKA INFO |language=}} {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|260px]]
Worship Hall (National Treasure) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=260}} | location = Katakura 645, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} | Position = {{coord|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | alternate name = Kamitani Hachiman Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = First Sunday of October | ceremony = }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine Gate}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, also 櫻井神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Katakura, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a [[Shikinaisha|shrine listed in the Engishiki]] and was formerly classified as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is also known as "Kamitani Hachiman Shrine." The worship hall is the only [[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]] in Sakai City. == Deities == The current deities enshrined are as followsShrine History.: * Central Hall: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} - the 15th emperor. * East Hall: {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} - the 14th emperor. * West Hall: {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} - consort of Emperor Chūai. == History == According to legend, the Sakurai clan, who lived in this area, enshrined their ancestor [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], marking the shrine's origin. In the 5th year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (597), the shrine incorporated a Hachiman shrine. In the [[Engishiki]] Jinmyōchō (927), the shrine is listed as "Sakurai Shrine" in [[Ōtori District]], {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}, indicating its status as a minor shrine. The shrine also later incorporated Yama no I Shrine and Kuni Shrine (a small shrine still exists near [[Hōdōji Temple]]), both listed in the Engishiki. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] progressed, leading to the construction of Kiyuzan Jingū-ji Temple within the shrine grounds. In the 4th year of the Kenmu era (1337), Kamitani Jōsu built a shrine building. In the 5th year of the [[Tenshō era]] (1577), the [[Siege of Kishū]] by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} resulted in the destruction of all buildings except the worship hall, and the confiscation of shrine lands. The reconstruction began in the 16th year of Tenshō (1588) by {{ill|Katō Kiyomasa|en|Katō Kiyomasa|WD=Q1069843|lt=Katō Kiyomasa}}, starting with the Amida Hall, and continued through the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1688–1704). In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1868), during the [[Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order]], Buddhist images and scriptures were moved to Konpuku-ji Temple in Katakura, and the Amida Hall was relocated to Hokke-ji Temple in Bessho. The bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storage building. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. Between 1908 (Meiji 41) and 1910 (Meiji 43), the shrine incorporated nine other shrines, including Kuni Shrine. In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] {{ill|Izumigaoka Station|en|Izumigaoka Station|WD=Q6102358|lt=Izumigaoka Station}}, take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", {{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=The Asahi Shimbun}}, 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sakurai Shrine (Q11537864)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ōtori district|ja|大鳥郡|zh|大鳥郡|WD=Q3270651}} || {{ill|Sakurai Shrine|lt=Sakurai Shrine|en|Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)|ja|桜井神社 (堺市)|commons|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)|fr|Sakurai-jinja|WD=Q11537864}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.485111|135.506270|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180988 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

In the 5th year of the [[Tenshō era]] (1577), the [[Siege of Kishū]] by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} resulted in the destruction of all buildings except the worship hall, and the confiscation of shrine lands. The reconstruction began in the 16th year of Tenshō (1588) by {{ill|Katō Kiyomasa|en|Katō Kiyomasa|WD=Q1069843|lt=Katō Kiyomasa}}, starting with the Amida Hall, and continued through the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1688–1704). In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1868), during the [[Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order]], Buddhist images and scriptures were moved to Konpuku-ji Temple in Katakura, and the Amida Hall was relocated to Hokke-ji Temple in Bessho. The bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storage building. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. Between 1908 (Meiji 41) and 1910 (Meiji 43), the shrine incorporated nine other shrines, including Kuni Shrine. In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] {{ill|Izumigaoka Station|en|Izumigaoka Station|WD=Q6102358|lt=Izumigaoka Station}}, take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", {{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=The Asahi Shimbun}}, 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sakurai Shrine (Q11537864)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ōtori district|ja|大鳥郡|zh|大鳥郡|WD=Q3270651}} || {{ill|Sakurai Shrine|lt=Sakurai Shrine|en|Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)|ja|桜井神社 (堺市)|commons|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)|fr|Sakurai-jinja|WD=Q11537864}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.485111|135.506270|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180988 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] { {{Short description|Shrine in Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Multiple issues| {{more citations needed|date=February 2024}} {{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=桜井神社 (堺市)|date=December 2021}} }}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | native_name = 桜井神社 | native_name_lang = ja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sakurai-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Worship Hall ({{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}) | location = 645 Katakura, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka}} 590-0121 | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | festival = First Sunday of October | country = {{flag|Japan}} | type = Former [[Modern_system_of_Ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|Ken-sha]] | prefecture= {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1041996}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.485417|N|135.506167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, ''Sakurai jinja'', also 櫻井神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It was founded at an unknown date and holds its festival on the first Sunday in October. It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} as [[kami]]. The oratory (拝殿, ''haiden'') is designated as the only {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=Japanese National Treasure}} in Sakai City.{{Cite web |title=Temples & Shrines |url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/foreign-language/english/visitors/enjoying/temples_shirines.html |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=www.city.sakai.lg.jp |language=}}{{Cite web |title=Sakurai Shrine |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/spot/sakuraijinja |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=OSAKA INFO |language=}} {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|260px]]
Worship Hall (National Treasure) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=260}} | location = Katakura 645, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} | Position = {{coord|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | alternate name = Kamitani Hachiman Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = First Sunday of October | ceremony = }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine Gate}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, also 櫻井神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Katakura, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a [[Shikinaisha|shrine listed in the Engishiki]] and was formerly classified as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is also known as "Kamitani Hachiman Shrine." The worship hall is the only [[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]] in Sakai City. == Deities == The current deities enshrined are as followsShrine History.: * Central Hall: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} - the 15th emperor. * East Hall: {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} - the 14th emperor. * West Hall: {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} - consort of Emperor Chūai. == History == According to legend, the Sakurai clan, who lived in this area, enshrined their ancestor [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], marking the shrine's origin. In the 5th year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (597), the shrine incorporated a Hachiman shrine. In the [[Engishiki]] Jinmyōchō (927), the shrine is listed as "Sakurai Shrine" in [[Ōtori District]], {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}, indicating its status as a minor shrine. The shrine also later incorporated Yama no I Shrine and Kuni Shrine (a small shrine still exists near [[Hōdōji Temple]]), both listed in the Engishiki. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] progressed, leading to the construction of Kiyuzan Jingū-ji Temple within the shrine grounds. In the 4th year of the Kenmu era (1337), Kamitani Jōsu built a shrine building. In the 5th year of the [[Tenshō era]] (1577), the [[Siege of Kishū]] by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} resulted in the destruction of all buildings except the worship hall, and the confiscation of shrine lands. The reconstruction began in the 16th year of Tenshō (1588) by {{ill|Katō Kiyomasa|en|Katō Kiyomasa|WD=Q1069843|lt=Katō Kiyomasa}}, starting with the Amida Hall, and continued through the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1688–1704). In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1868), during the [[Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order]], Buddhist images and scriptures were moved to Konpuku-ji Temple in Katakura, and the Amida Hall was relocated to Hokke-ji Temple in Bessho. The bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storage building. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. Between 1908 (Meiji 41) and 1910 (Meiji 43), the shrine incorporated nine other shrines, including Kuni Shrine. In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] {{ill|Izumigaoka Station|en|Izumigaoka Station|WD=Q6102358|lt=Izumigaoka Station}}, take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", {{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=The Asahi Shimbun}}, 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sakurai Shrine (Q11537864)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ōtori district|ja|大鳥郡|zh|大鳥郡|WD=Q3270651}} || {{ill|Sakurai Shrine|lt=Sakurai Shrine|en|Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)|ja|桜井神社 (堺市)|commons|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)|fr|Sakurai-jinja|WD=Q11537864}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.485111|135.506270|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180988 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=桜井神社 | 画像=[[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
拝殿（[[国宝]]） | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[南区 (堺市)|南区]]片蔵645 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]]
[[仲哀天皇]]
[[神功皇后]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[府社]] | 創建=不詳 | 別名=上神谷八幡宮 | 札所等= | 例祭=10月第1日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=桜井神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|神門}}]] '''桜井神社'''（さくらいじんじゃ、'''櫻井神社'''）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[南区 (堺市)|南区]]片蔵にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[府社]]。別称は「上神谷八幡宮」（にわだにはちまんぐう）とも。拝殿は堺市内唯一の[[国宝]]に指定されている。 == 祭神 == 現在の祭神は次の3柱神社由緒書。。 * 中殿：[[応神天皇]] - 第15代天皇。 * 東殿：[[仲哀天皇]] - 第14代天皇。 * 西殿：[[神功皇后]] - 仲哀天皇皇后。 == 歴史 == 伝承によると、この地方に住居していた桜井朝臣の一族がその祖先である[[武内宿禰]]を奉斎したのが創祀という。また[[推古天皇]]5年（[[597年]]）には[[八幡宮]]を合祀したという。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[和泉国]][[大鳥郡]]に「桜井神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している。また、後世に当社に合祀された山井神社・国神社（[[法道寺 (堺市)|法道寺]]の東にあった。現在も小さな社がある）もそれぞれ式内社に列している。 [[平安時代]]には[[神仏習合]]が進み、亀遊山神宮寺が境内に建てられた。[[建武 (日本)|建武]]4年（[[1337年]]）、上神城主の上神常儀が社殿を造営している。 [[天正]]5年（[[1577年]]）、[[織田信長]]による[[紀州征伐]]の戦火を受けて拝殿以外を焼失し、社領も没収される。 天正16年（[[1588年]]）に[[加藤清正]]によって阿弥陀堂が再建されたのをかわきりに、[[元禄]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1704年]]）までに復興が行われた。 [[慶応]]4年（[[1868年]]）[[神仏分離]]に際し、仏像や経典などは片蔵の金福寺に移され、阿弥陀堂は別所の法華寺へ移築された。また、後に鐘楼は神輿庫に改造されている。 [[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列せられた。また、[[1908年]]（明治41年）から[[1910年]]（明治43年）にかけて国神社ほか9社を合祀している。 [[1942年]]（[[昭和]]17年）には[[府社]]に昇格した。 == 境内 == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|拝殿背面}}]] * 本殿 - [[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）再建。 * 幣殿 * 中門 - [[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）建立。 * 拝殿（割拝殿、[[国宝]]） - [[鎌倉時代]]前期の建立。切妻造、本瓦葺き。寺院建築風の簡素な意匠の拝殿である。柱間は桁行（正面）5間、梁間（側面）3間。桁行5間のうち中央の間を馬道（めどう、通路の意）とし、左右を部屋とする。このような形式の拝殿を割拝殿といい、拝殿の中央が土間のように通り抜けができる。ただし、これは当初からの形式ではなく、建立当初は全面に床を張り、建物周囲には縁をめぐらせていたことが痕跡からわかる。正面は馬道部分を除いて桟唐戸（さんからと）を吊り、側面は二重虹梁蟇股（にじゅうこうりょうかえるまた）{{efn|「梁」は、柱と柱とを、棟と直交方向につなぐ水平材。中央部を高く、両端をわずかに湾曲させた形状のものを「虹梁」という。蟇股は、梁などの上に置かれる構造材で、[[カエル]]が脚を広げたような形状からその名がある。}}とする。内部は天井を張らず、梁、垂木などの構造材をそのまま見せた「化粧屋根裏」とする。神社の拝殿は建築年代の古いものが少なく、桜井神社拝殿は鎌倉時代にさかのぼる例として貴重であるとして、国宝に指定されている（背面の向拝部分は国宝指定外）。 * 絵馬殿 * 神輿庫 - [[享保]]16年（[[1731年]]）に鐘楼として建立されたもの。明治時代に神輿庫に改造された。 * 社務所 * 神門 - [[元禄]]15年（[[1702年]]）再建。 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|本殿・中門 == 摂末社 == * 白福稲荷社 - 祭神：[[保食神]] * 戎社 * 招魂社 File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|白福稲荷社 File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|戎社 File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|招魂社 == 祭事 == 10月第1日曜日の秋祭に「上神谷のこおどり」が奉納される。 == 文化財 == === 国宝 === * 拝殿（建造物） - [[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）4月5日に[[古社寺保存法]]に基づき特別保護建造物に指定。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）11月14日に[[文化財保護法]]に基づき国宝に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2130|桜井神社拝殿}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html 桜井神社拝殿]（堺市ホームページ）。。 === 選択無形民俗文化財（国選択） === * 上神谷のこおどり - 元は国神社へ奉納する踊り。[[1972年]]（昭和47年）8月5日選択。堺こおどり保存会。 === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 木造神像（彫刻） - 元は国神社に祀られていた神像。[[1982年]]（昭和57年）3月31日指定[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成29年6月7日更新版）の堺市ファイルより。[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html 神像]（堺市ホームページ）。。 *** 男神像 *** 菩薩形像 *** 明王形像 ** 石造燈籠（工芸品） - 元は国神社にあった燈籠。[[応永]]19年（[[1412年]]）作。[[1970年]]（昭和45年）2月20日指定[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html 燈籠]（堺市ホームページ）。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 上神谷のこおどり == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * 大阪府堺市南区片蔵645 '''交通アクセス''' * [[南海泉北線]] [[泉ケ丘駅]]から南海バス乗車（23系統・「畑」方面）桜井神社前で下車すぐ、または（24系統・「鉢ケ峯」方面）片蔵で下車、徒歩約5分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * 『週刊朝日百科』「日本の国宝 35」、[[朝日新聞社]]、1997 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 櫻井神社 堺観光ガイド] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja 櫻井神社 OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ 櫻井神社 JRおでかけネット] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]]

revision.comment: Merged Japanese Wikipedia content with full revision history and English content
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] { {{Short description|Shrine in Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Multiple issues| {{more citations needed|date=February 2024}} {{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=桜井神社 (堺市)|date=December 2021}} }}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | native_name = 桜井神社 | native_name_lang = ja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sakurai-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Worship Hall ({{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}) | location = 645 Katakura, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka}} 590-0121 | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | festival = First Sunday of October | country = {{flag|Japan}} | type = Former [[Modern_system_of_Ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|Ken-sha]] | prefecture= {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1041996}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.485417|N|135.506167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, ''Sakurai jinja'', also 櫻井神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It was founded at an unknown date and holds its festival on the first Sunday in October. It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} as [[kami]]. The oratory (拝殿, ''haiden'') is designated as the only {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=Japanese National Treasure}} in Sakai City.{{Cite web |title=Temples & Shrines |url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/foreign-language/english/visitors/enjoying/temples_shirines.html |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=www.city.sakai.lg.jp |language=}}{{Cite web |title=Sakurai Shrine |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/spot/sakuraijinja |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=OSAKA INFO |language=}} {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|260px]]
Worship Hall (National Treasure) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=260}} | location = Katakura 645, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} | Position = {{coord|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | alternate name = Kamitani Hachiman Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = First Sunday of October | ceremony = }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine Gate}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, also 櫻井神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Katakura, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a [[Shikinaisha|shrine listed in the Engishiki]] and was formerly classified as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is also known as "Kamitani Hachiman Shrine." The worship hall is the only [[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]] in Sakai City. == Deities == The current deities enshrined are as followsShrine History.: * Central Hall: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} - the 15th emperor. * East Hall: {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} - the 14th emperor. * West Hall: {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} - consort of Emperor Chūai. == History == According to legend, the Sakurai clan, who lived in this area, enshrined their ancestor [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], marking the shrine's origin. In the 5th year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (597), the shrine incorporated a Hachiman shrine. In the [[Engishiki]] Jinmyōchō (927), the shrine is listed as "Sakurai Shrine" in [[Ōtori District]], {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}, indicating its status as a minor shrine. The shrine also later incorporated Yama no I Shrine and Kuni Shrine (a small shrine still exists near [[Hōdōji Temple]]), both listed in the Engishiki. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] progressed, leading to the construction of Kiyuzan Jingū-ji Temple within the shrine grounds. In the 4th year of the Kenmu era (1337), Kamitani Jōsu built a shrine building. In the 5th year of the [[Tenshō era]] (1577), the [[Siege of Kishū]] by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} resulted in the destruction of all buildings except the worship hall, and the confiscation of shrine lands. The reconstruction began in the 16th year of Tenshō (1588) by {{ill|Katō Kiyomasa|en|Katō Kiyomasa|WD=Q1069843|lt=Katō Kiyomasa}}, starting with the Amida Hall, and continued through the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1688–1704). In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1868), during the [[Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order]], Buddhist images and scriptures were moved to Konpuku-ji Temple in Katakura, and the Amida Hall was relocated to Hokke-ji Temple in Bessho. The bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storage building. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. Between 1908 (Meiji 41) and 1910 (Meiji 43), the shrine incorporated nine other shrines, including Kuni Shrine. In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] {{ill|Izumigaoka Station|en|Izumigaoka Station|WD=Q6102358|lt=Izumigaoka Station}}, take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", {{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=The Asahi Shimbun}}, 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sakurai Shrine (Q11537864)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ōtori district|ja|大鳥郡|zh|大鳥郡|WD=Q3270651}} || {{ill|Sakurai Shrine|lt=Sakurai Shrine|en|Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)|ja|桜井神社 (堺市)|commons|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)|fr|Sakurai-jinja|WD=Q11537864}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.485111|135.506270|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180988 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{translated page|ja|桜井神社 (堺市)|version=107249593|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 | 名称=桜井神社 | 画像=[[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
拝殿（[[国宝]]） | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[南区 (堺市)|南区]]片蔵645 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]]
[[仲哀天皇]]
[[神功皇后]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[府社]] | 創建=不詳 | 別名=上神谷八幡宮 | 札所等= | 例祭=10月第1日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=桜井神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|神門}}]] '''桜井神社'''（さくらいじんじゃ、'''櫻井神社'''）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[南区 (堺市)|南区]]片蔵にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[府社]]。別称は「上神谷八幡宮」（にわだにはちまんぐう）とも。拝殿は堺市内唯一の[[国宝]]に指定されている。 == 祭神 == 現在の祭神は次の3柱神社由緒書。。 * 中殿：[[応神天皇]] - 第15代天皇。 * 東殿：[[仲哀天皇]] - 第14代天皇。 * 西殿：[[神功皇后]] - 仲哀天皇皇后。 == 歴史 == 伝承によると、この地方に住居していた桜井朝臣の一族がその祖先である[[武内宿禰]]を奉斎したのが創祀という。また[[推古天皇]]5年（[[597年]]）には[[八幡宮]]を合祀したという。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[和泉国]][[大鳥郡]]に「桜井神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している。また、後世に当社に合祀された山井神社・国神社（[[法道寺 (堺市)|法道寺]]の東にあった。現在も小さな社がある）もそれぞれ式内社に列している。 [[平安時代]]には[[神仏習合]]が進み、亀遊山神宮寺が境内に建てられた。[[建武 (日本)|建武]]4年（[[1337年]]）、上神城主の上神常儀が社殿を造営している。 [[天正]]5年（[[1577年]]）、[[織田信長]]による[[紀州征伐]]の戦火を受けて拝殿以外を焼失し、社領も没収される。 天正16年（[[1588年]]）に[[加藤清正]]によって阿弥陀堂が再建されたのをかわきりに、[[元禄]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1704年]]）までに復興が行われた。 [[慶応]]4年（[[1868年]]）[[神仏分離]]に際し、仏像や経典などは片蔵の金福寺に移され、阿弥陀堂は別所の法華寺へ移築された。また、後に鐘楼は神輿庫に改造されている。 [[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列せられた。また、[[1908年]]（明治41年）から[[1910年]]（明治43年）にかけて国神社ほか9社を合祀している。 [[1942年]]（[[昭和]]17年）には[[府社]]に昇格した。 == 境内 == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|拝殿背面}}]] * 本殿 - [[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）再建。 * 幣殿 * 中門 - [[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）建立。 * 拝殿（割拝殿、[[国宝]]） - [[鎌倉時代]]前期の建立。切妻造、本瓦葺き。寺院建築風の簡素な意匠の拝殿である。柱間は桁行（正面）5間、梁間（側面）3間。桁行5間のうち中央の間を馬道（めどう、通路の意）とし、左右を部屋とする。このような形式の拝殿を割拝殿といい、拝殿の中央が土間のように通り抜けができる。ただし、これは当初からの形式ではなく、建立当初は全面に床を張り、建物周囲には縁をめぐらせていたことが痕跡からわかる。正面は馬道部分を除いて桟唐戸（さんからと）を吊り、側面は二重虹梁蟇股（にじゅうこうりょうかえるまた）{{efn|「梁」は、柱と柱とを、棟と直交方向につなぐ水平材。中央部を高く、両端をわずかに湾曲させた形状のものを「虹梁」という。蟇股は、梁などの上に置かれる構造材で、[[カエル]]が脚を広げたような形状からその名がある。}}とする。内部は天井を張らず、梁、垂木などの構造材をそのまま見せた「化粧屋根裏」とする。神社の拝殿は建築年代の古いものが少なく、桜井神社拝殿は鎌倉時代にさかのぼる例として貴重であるとして、国宝に指定されている（背面の向拝部分は国宝指定外）。 * 絵馬殿 * 神輿庫 - [[享保]]16年（[[1731年]]）に鐘楼として建立されたもの。明治時代に神輿庫に改造された。 * 社務所 * 神門 - [[元禄]]15年（[[1702年]]）再建。 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|本殿・中門 == 摂末社 == * 白福稲荷社 - 祭神：[[保食神]] * 戎社 * 招魂社 File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|白福稲荷社 File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|戎社 File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|招魂社 == 祭事 == 10月第1日曜日の秋祭に「上神谷のこおどり」が奉納される。 == 文化財 == === 国宝 === * 拝殿（建造物） - [[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）4月5日に[[古社寺保存法]]に基づき特別保護建造物に指定。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）11月14日に[[文化財保護法]]に基づき国宝に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2130|桜井神社拝殿}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html 桜井神社拝殿]（堺市ホームページ）。。 === 選択無形民俗文化財（国選択） === * 上神谷のこおどり - 元は国神社へ奉納する踊り。[[1972年]]（昭和47年）8月5日選択。堺こおどり保存会。 === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 木造神像（彫刻） - 元は国神社に祀られていた神像。[[1982年]]（昭和57年）3月31日指定[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成29年6月7日更新版）の堺市ファイルより。[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html 神像]（堺市ホームページ）。。 *** 男神像 *** 菩薩形像 *** 明王形像 ** 石造燈籠（工芸品） - 元は国神社にあった燈籠。[[応永]]19年（[[1412年]]）作。[[1970年]]（昭和45年）2月20日指定[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html 燈籠]（堺市ホームページ）。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 上神谷のこおどり == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * 大阪府堺市南区片蔵645 '''交通アクセス''' * [[南海泉北線]] [[泉ケ丘駅]]から南海バス乗車（23系統・「畑」方面）桜井神社前で下車すぐ、または（24系統・「鉢ケ峯」方面）片蔵で下車、徒歩約5分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * 『週刊朝日百科』「日本の国宝 35」、[[朝日新聞社]]、1997 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 櫻井神社 堺観光ガイド] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja 櫻井神社 OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ 櫻井神社 JRおでかけネット] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] ==End Japanese==

[[co:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)]][[en:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]][[fr:Sakurai-jinja]][[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] { {{Short description|Shrine in Sakai, Osaka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Multiple issues| {{more citations needed|date=February 2024}} {{Expand language|topic=|langcode=ja|otherarticle=桜井神社 (堺市)|date=December 2021}} }}{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | native_name = 桜井神社 | native_name_lang = ja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Sakurai-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = Worship Hall ({{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure}}) | location = 645 Katakura, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka}} 590-0121 | deity = {{ill|Chūai|WD=Q179971}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | festival = First Sunday of October | country = {{flag|Japan}} | type = Former [[Modern_system_of_Ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|Ken-sha]] | prefecture= {{ill|Minami-ku|WD=Q1041996}} | coordinates= {{Coord|34.485417|N|135.506167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, ''Sakurai jinja'', also 櫻井神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It was founded at an unknown date and holds its festival on the first Sunday in October. It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}, and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} as [[kami]]. The oratory (拝殿, ''haiden'') is designated as the only {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|WD=Q1139795|lt=Japanese National Treasure}} in Sakai City.{{Cite web |title=Temples & Shrines |url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/foreign-language/english/visitors/enjoying/temples_shirines.html |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=www.city.sakai.lg.jp |language=}}{{Cite web |title=Sakurai Shrine |url=https://osaka-info.jp/en/spot/sakuraijinja |access-date=2024-02-09 |website=OSAKA INFO |language=}} {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|260px]]
Worship Hall (National Treasure) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=260}} | location = Katakura 645, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} | Position = {{coord|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded = Unknown | alternate name = Kamitani Hachiman Shrine | Pilgrimage = | festival = First Sunday of October | ceremony = }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shrine Gate}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (桜井神社, also 櫻井神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Katakura, {{ill|Minami-ku, Sakai|en|Minami-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami-ku}}, {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a [[Shikinaisha|shrine listed in the Engishiki]] and was formerly classified as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is also known as "Kamitani Hachiman Shrine." The worship hall is the only [[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]] in Sakai City. == Deities == The current deities enshrined are as followsShrine History.: * Central Hall: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} - the 15th emperor. * East Hall: {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} - the 14th emperor. * West Hall: {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} - consort of Emperor Chūai. == History == According to legend, the Sakurai clan, who lived in this area, enshrined their ancestor [[Takenouchi no Sukune]], marking the shrine's origin. In the 5th year of the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} (597), the shrine incorporated a Hachiman shrine. In the [[Engishiki]] Jinmyōchō (927), the shrine is listed as "Sakurai Shrine" in [[Ōtori District]], {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}}, indicating its status as a minor shrine. The shrine also later incorporated Yama no I Shrine and Kuni Shrine (a small shrine still exists near [[Hōdōji Temple]]), both listed in the Engishiki. During the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, [[syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism]] progressed, leading to the construction of Kiyuzan Jingū-ji Temple within the shrine grounds. In the 4th year of the Kenmu era (1337), Kamitani Jōsu built a shrine building. In the 5th year of the [[Tenshō era]] (1577), the [[Siege of Kishū]] by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} resulted in the destruction of all buildings except the worship hall, and the confiscation of shrine lands. The reconstruction began in the 16th year of Tenshō (1588) by {{ill|Katō Kiyomasa|en|Katō Kiyomasa|WD=Q1069843|lt=Katō Kiyomasa}}, starting with the Amida Hall, and continued through the [[:en:Genroku era|Genroku era]] (1688–1704). In the 4th year of the Keiō era (1868), during the [[Shinto and Buddhism Separation Order]], Buddhist images and scriptures were moved to Konpuku-ji Temple in Katakura, and the Amida Hall was relocated to Hokke-ji Temple in Bessho. The bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storage building. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system. Between 1908 (Meiji 41) and 1910 (Meiji 43), the shrine incorporated nine other shrines, including Kuni Shrine. In 1942 (Shōwa 17), it was promoted to a prefectural shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear of the Worship Hall}}]] * main hall - Rebuilt in 1881 (Meiji 14). * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Middle Gate - Built in 1931 (Shōwa 6). * Worship Hall (割拝殿, National Treasure) - Built in the early Kamakura period. Gabled roof, tile roofing. The hall features a simple design resembling temple architecture. The columns span 5 bays in width and 3 bays in depth. The central bay serves as a passage (medō), with rooms on either side. This style of hall is known as waritsuke-haiden, allowing passage through the center. The original design featured a floored interior with surrounding verandas, which were later removed. The interior is open to the rafters and beams, creating an exposed framework. It is designated as a National Treasure due to its rarity and historical significance from the Kamakura period. * Ema Hall (Votive Picture Hall) * Mikoshi Storage - Originally built as a bell tower in 1731 (Kyōhō 16), later converted into a mikoshi storage in the Meiji era. * Shrine Office * Shrine Gate - Rebuilt in 1702 (Genroku 15). File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|main hall and Middle Gate == Sub-shrines == * Shirofuku Inari Shrine - deity: [[Ukanomitama no Kami]] * Ebisu Shrine * Shōkonsha Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shōkonsha Shrine == festivals == The "Kamitani Koodori" is performed during the autumn festival on the first Sunday of October. == Cultural Properties == === National Treasure === * Worship Hall (Building) - Designated as a Special Protected Building under the [[Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act]] on April 5, 1917 (Taishō 6). Re-designated as a National Treasure under the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] on November 14, 1953 (Shōwa 28){{National Cultural Properties Database|102|2130|Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html Sakurai Shrine Worship Hall] (Sakai City Website).. === Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property (Nationally Selected) === * Kamitani Koodori - Originally dedicated to Kuni Shrine. Selected on August 5, 1972 (Shōwa 47). Preserved by the Sakai Koodori Preservation Society. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden Statues (Sculptures) - Originally enshrined in Kuni Shrine. Designated on March 31, 1982 (Shōwa 57)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Osaka Prefecture Website, updated June 7, 2017).[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html Statues] (Sakai City Website).. *** Male deity Statue *** Bodhisattva Statue *** Myōō Statue ** Stone Lantern (Craft) - Originally from Kuni Shrine. Made in the 19th year of the Ōei era (1412). Designated on February 20, 1970 (Shōwa 45)[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html Lantern] (Sakai City Website).. * Intangible Folk Cultural Property ** Kamitani Koodori == Information == '''location''' * Katakura 645, Minami-ku, Sakai, Osaka Prefecture '''Access''' * From [[:en:Senboku Rapid Railway|Senboku Rapid Railway]] {{ill|Izumigaoka Station|en|Izumigaoka Station|WD=Q6102358|lt=Izumigaoka Station}}, take the Nankai Bus (Line 23 to Hatake), get off at Sakurai Shrine-mae, or (Line 24 to Hachiga-mine), get off at Katakura, and walk for about 5 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine History * 'Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia' "National Treasures of Japan 35", {{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|WD=Q720503|lt=The Asahi Shimbun}}, 1997 == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sakurai Shrine Sakai Tourism Guide] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja Sakurai Shrine OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ Sakurai Shrine JR Odekake Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] == References == ==External links== *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 Sightseeing website] {{coord|34.4852|135.5063|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[en:Sakurai Shrine]] {{Commons category|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[fr:Sakurai-jinja]] [[ja:桜井神社 (堺市)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sakurai Shrine (Q11537864)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Ōtori district|ja|大鳥郡|zh|大鳥郡|WD=Q3270651}} || {{ill|Sakurai Shrine|lt=Sakurai Shrine|en|Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)|ja|桜井神社 (堺市)|commons|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)|fr|Sakurai-jinja|WD=Q11537864}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.485111|135.506270|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180988 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} [[Category:Fuken-sha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{translated page|ja|桜井神社 (堺市)|version=107249593|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 | 名称=桜井神社 | 画像=[[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
拝殿（[[国宝]]） | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[南区 (堺市)|南区]]片蔵645 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]]
[[仲哀天皇]]
[[神功皇后]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[府社]] | 創建=不詳 | 別名=上神谷八幡宮 | 札所等= | 例祭=10月第1日曜日 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=桜井神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|神門}}]] '''桜井神社'''（さくらいじんじゃ、'''櫻井神社'''）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]][[南区 (堺市)|南区]]片蔵にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[府社]]。別称は「上神谷八幡宮」（にわだにはちまんぐう）とも。拝殿は堺市内唯一の[[国宝]]に指定されている。 == 祭神 == 現在の祭神は次の3柱神社由緒書。。 * 中殿：[[応神天皇]] - 第15代天皇。 * 東殿：[[仲哀天皇]] - 第14代天皇。 * 西殿：[[神功皇后]] - 仲哀天皇皇后。 == 歴史 == 伝承によると、この地方に住居していた桜井朝臣の一族がその祖先である[[武内宿禰]]を奉斎したのが創祀という。また[[推古天皇]]5年（[[597年]]）には[[八幡宮]]を合祀したという。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では[[和泉国]][[大鳥郡]]に「桜井神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している。また、後世に当社に合祀された山井神社・国神社（[[法道寺 (堺市)|法道寺]]の東にあった。現在も小さな社がある）もそれぞれ式内社に列している。 [[平安時代]]には[[神仏習合]]が進み、亀遊山神宮寺が境内に建てられた。[[建武 (日本)|建武]]4年（[[1337年]]）、上神城主の上神常儀が社殿を造営している。 [[天正]]5年（[[1577年]]）、[[織田信長]]による[[紀州征伐]]の戦火を受けて拝殿以外を焼失し、社領も没収される。 天正16年（[[1588年]]）に[[加藤清正]]によって阿弥陀堂が再建されたのをかわきりに、[[元禄]]年間（[[1688年]] - [[1704年]]）までに復興が行われた。 [[慶応]]4年（[[1868年]]）[[神仏分離]]に際し、仏像や経典などは片蔵の金福寺に移され、阿弥陀堂は別所の法華寺へ移築された。また、後に鐘楼は神輿庫に改造されている。 [[1872年]]（[[明治]]5年）に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列せられた。また、[[1908年]]（明治41年）から[[1910年]]（明治43年）にかけて国神社ほか9社を合祀している。 [[1942年]]（[[昭和]]17年）には[[府社]]に昇格した。 == 境内 == [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|拝殿背面}}]] * 本殿 - [[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）再建。 * 幣殿 * 中門 - [[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）建立。 * 拝殿（割拝殿、[[国宝]]） - [[鎌倉時代]]前期の建立。切妻造、本瓦葺き。寺院建築風の簡素な意匠の拝殿である。柱間は桁行（正面）5間、梁間（側面）3間。桁行5間のうち中央の間を馬道（めどう、通路の意）とし、左右を部屋とする。このような形式の拝殿を割拝殿といい、拝殿の中央が土間のように通り抜けができる。ただし、これは当初からの形式ではなく、建立当初は全面に床を張り、建物周囲には縁をめぐらせていたことが痕跡からわかる。正面は馬道部分を除いて桟唐戸（さんからと）を吊り、側面は二重虹梁蟇股（にじゅうこうりょうかえるまた）{{efn|「梁」は、柱と柱とを、棟と直交方向につなぐ水平材。中央部を高く、両端をわずかに湾曲させた形状のものを「虹梁」という。蟇股は、梁などの上に置かれる構造材で、[[カエル]]が脚を広げたような形状からその名がある。}}とする。内部は天井を張らず、梁、垂木などの構造材をそのまま見せた「化粧屋根裏」とする。神社の拝殿は建築年代の古いものが少なく、桜井神社拝殿は鎌倉時代にさかのぼる例として貴重であるとして、国宝に指定されている（背面の向拝部分は国宝指定外）。 * 絵馬殿 * 神輿庫 - [[享保]]16年（[[1731年]]）に鐘楼として建立されたもの。明治時代に神輿庫に改造された。 * 社務所 * 神門 - [[元禄]]15年（[[1702年]]）再建。 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|本殿・中門 == 摂末社 == * 白福稲荷社 - 祭神：[[保食神]] * 戎社 * 招魂社 File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|白福稲荷社 File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|戎社 File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|招魂社 == 祭事 == 10月第1日曜日の秋祭に「上神谷のこおどり」が奉納される。 == 文化財 == === 国宝 === * 拝殿（建造物） - [[1917年]]（[[大正]]6年）4月5日に[[古社寺保存法]]に基づき特別保護建造物に指定。[[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）11月14日に[[文化財保護法]]に基づき国宝に指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|2130|桜井神社拝殿}}[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kenzobutu/sakuraijinja.html 桜井神社拝殿]（堺市ホームページ）。。 === 選択無形民俗文化財（国選択） === * 上神谷のこおどり - 元は国神社へ奉納する踊り。[[1972年]]（昭和47年）8月5日選択。堺こおどり保存会。 === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 木造神像（彫刻） - 元は国神社に祀られていた神像。[[1982年]]（昭和57年）3月31日指定[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成29年6月7日更新版）の堺市ファイルより。[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/cyokoku/shinzo.html 神像]（堺市ホームページ）。。 *** 男神像 *** 菩薩形像 *** 明王形像 ** 石造燈籠（工芸品） - 元は国神社にあった燈籠。[[応永]]19年（[[1412年]]）作。[[1970年]]（昭和45年）2月20日指定[http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/kougeihin/toro.html 燈籠]（堺市ホームページ）。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 上神谷のこおどり == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * 大阪府堺市南区片蔵645 '''交通アクセス''' * [[南海泉北線]] [[泉ケ丘駅]]から南海バス乗車（23系統・「畑」方面）桜井神社前で下車すぐ、または（24系統・「鉢ケ峯」方面）片蔵で下車、徒歩約5分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * 『週刊朝日百科』「日本の国宝 35」、[[朝日新聞社]]、1997 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} *[https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/75 櫻井神社 堺観光ガイド] *[https://osaka-info.jp/page/sakuraijinja 櫻井神社 OSAKA-INFO] *[https://guide.jr-odekake.net/smt/spot/4120/ 櫻井神社 JRおでかけネット] {{神道 横}} [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] ==End Japanese==
