(P9721) == * Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg {{commons category|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[EN:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]] [[FR:Sakurai-jinja]] [[JA:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}}) | location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|fusha}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{jalink|Sakai|Q193428}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} * East hall: {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}} * West hall: {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:need translation]]

(P9721) == * Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg {{commons category|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[EN:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]] [[FR:Sakurai-jinja]] [[JA:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}}) | location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|fusha}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{jalink|Sakai|Q193428}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} * East hall: {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}} * West hall: {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]] [[Category:need translation]]

Haiden ({{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}}) | location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|fusha}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{jalink|Sakai|Q193428}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} * East hall: {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}} * West hall: {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]]

(P9721) == * Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg {{commons category|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[EN:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]] [[FR:Sakurai-jinja]] [[JA:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}}) | location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|fusha}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{jalink|Sakai|Q193428}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} * East hall: {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}} * West hall: {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]]

(P9721) == * Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg {{commons category|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[EN:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]] [[FR:Sakurai-jinja]] [[JA:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}}) | location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}
{{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}
{{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|fusha}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{jalink|Sakai|Q193428}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{jalink|National Treasure|Q1139795}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} * East hall: {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}} * West hall: {{jalink|Empress Jingu|Q232803}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{jalink|Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}}) | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}{{ill|lt=Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} | status = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill||lt=fusha|UNKNOWN}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} * East hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} * West hall: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(P9721) == * Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg {{commons category|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[EN:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]] [[FR:Sakurai-jinja]] [[JA:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}}) | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}{{ill|lt=Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=fusha|UNKNOWN}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} * East hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} * West hall: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}}) | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}{{ill|lt=Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=fusha|1=UNKNOWN}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} * East hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} * West hall: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

(P9721) == * Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg {{commons category|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[EN:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]] [[FR:Sakurai-jinja]] [[JA:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}}) | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}{{ill|lt=Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=fusha|1=UNKNOWN}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} * East hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} * West hall: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}}) | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}{{ill|lt=Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} * East hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} * West hall: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} * East hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}} * West hall: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * East hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * West hall: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Osaka), shinmon.jpg {{commons category|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[EN:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]] [[FR:Sakurai-jinja]] [[JA:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * East hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * West hall: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Osaka), shinmon.jpg {{commons category|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[EN:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]] [[FR:Sakurai-jinja]] [[JA:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * East hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * West hall: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらいしんしや さかいし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * East hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * West hall: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]]

Osaka), shinmon.jpg {{commons category|Category:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} [[EN:Sakurai Shrine (Sakai)]] [[FR:Sakurai-jinja]] [[JA:桜井神社 (堺市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11537864}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Sakurai Shrine | image = [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden ({{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}) | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Minami-ku, Sakai|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}} Katakura 645 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|29|7.5|N|135|30|22.2|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|Fu-sha|WD=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} | other_name = Niwadani Hachimangu | annual_festival = First Sunday of October }} [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), shinmon.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Shinmon}}]] '''Sakurai Shrine''' (さくらいじんじゃ / 櫻井神社) is a Shinto shrine in Katakura, Minami Ward, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, Osaka. It is a shikinaisha and formerly ranked as a fusha. Also known as "Niwadani Hachimangu," it is notable because its haiden is the only {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|WD=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} building in Sakai City. === Enshrined deities === * Central hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * East hall: {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * West hall: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === History === Tradition says the Sakurai-no-Ason clan of this area began the shrine by worshiping their ancestor {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}, and that a Hachiman deity was merged in 597. In the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho'' (927), "Sakurai Shrine" is listed in Otori District, Izumi Province. Yamai Shrine and Kuni Shrine, later merged into this shrine, were also listed as shikinaisha. In 1337, local lord Ueno Tsuneyoshi rebuilt shrine buildings. In 1577, war damage during Oda Nobunaga's Kishu campaign destroyed most buildings except the haiden, and shrine lands were confiscated. Reconstruction progressed from 1588 through the Genroku era. During shinbutsu bunri in 1868, Buddhist images and sutras were moved out; the Amida hall was relocated, and the bell tower was later converted into a mikoshi storehouse. The shrine became gosha in 1872 and was promoted to fusha in 1942. === Precinct === [[File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), haiden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Rear view of haiden}}]] * Honden - rebuilt 1881 * Heiden * Chumon - built 1931 * Haiden (wari-haiden, National Treasure) - early Kamakura period; kirizuma style with tiled roof * Emaden * Mikoshi storehouse (former bell tower built in 1731) * Shrine office * Shinmon - rebuilt 1702 File:Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka), honden.jpg|Honden and chumon === Subsidiary shrines === * Shirofuku Inari ({{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}) * Ebisu Shrine * Shokonsha File:Sakurai-jinja Inari.jpg|Shirofuku Inari File:Sakurai-jinja Ebisu.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Sakurai-jinja syokonsha.jpg|Shokonsha === Festivals === "Kami-Niwadani Koodori" is dedicated during the autumn festival (first Sunday of October). === Cultural properties === === National Treasure === * Haiden (building): designated as specially protected building in 1917; designated National Treasure under the Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties in 1953. === Nationally selected intangible folk cultural property === * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (selected 1972) === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Wooden deity statues (sculpture group) * Stone lantern (made 1412) * Kami-Niwadani no Koodori (intangible) === Access === From Nankai Semboku Line Izumigaoka Station, Nankai Bus: * Route 23 (toward Hata): get off at "Sakurai Jinja-mae" (immediate) * Route 24 (toward Hachigamine): get off at "Katakura" (about 5 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Explanatory notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === * Shrine origin record * ''Weekly Asahi Encyclopedia: National Treasures of Japan 35'' (Asahi Shimbun, 1997) === External links === {{Commonscat|Sakurai-jinja (Sakai, Osaka)}} * Sakurai Shrine pages on Sakai Tourism Guide, Osaka Info, and JR Odekake Net {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sakurai Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の旧府社]] [[Category:日本の国宝 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の国宝]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:鎌倉時代の建築]][[Category:qq]]
